title: ulmus × hollandica f. major
intro: Ulmus × hollandica f. major, commonly known as the English Elm, is a deciduous tree species that is native to Europe and western Asia. This plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in wound healing, skin protection, and immune system support. The therapeutic actions of Ulmus × hollandica f. major include anti-diarrheal, anti-spasmodic, and anti-ulcerative effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders and promote digestive health. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which contribute to its medicinal properties and make it a valuable resource for traditional medicine.
benefits: Ulmus × hollandica f. major has many benefits, such as promoting cardiovascular health through its high levels of antioxidants, reducing the risk of heart disease, and lowering blood pressure. The plant's extracts have also been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve respiratory function, and even help manage diabetes. Additionally, the leaves and bark of Ulmus × hollandica f. major have been used to create herbal remedies that can help soothe skin irritations, improve digestion, and even reduce stress levels, ultimately leading to improved overall well-being and a better quality of life. By incorporating this plant into one's daily life, individuals can reap a wide range of benefits that can have a significant impact on both physical and mental health.
constituents: Ulmus × hollandica f. major has many active constituents, such as flavonoids like quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, potentially alleviating symptoms of arthritis and improving heart health. The plant also contains phenolic acids, including ferulic acid and sinapic acid, which have been found to possess antimicrobial and antiviral properties, making them useful in the treatment of infections and certain diseases. Additionally, Ulmus × hollandica f. major contains various terpenoids, including ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, which have been demonstrated to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, potentially aiding in the management of conditions such as asthma and autoimmune disorders. By harnessing the medicinal properties of Ulmus × hollandica f. major, individuals can potentially improve their overall well-being, reducing their reliance on pharmaceuticals and enhancing their quality of life.
parts: Ulmus × hollandica f. major has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, flowers, and bark. The leaves of Ulmus × hollandica f. major contain medicinal constituents like salicylic acid, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The flowers of this plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The bark of Ulmus × hollandica f. major is also used medicinally and contains compounds like salicin, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
preparations: Ulmus × hollandica f. major has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties. The tea made from the bark of Ulmus × hollandica f. major is used to treat diarrhea and dysentery, while the tincture is employed to combat fever and inflammation. Infusions of the plant's leaves are used to soothe digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome, and to reduce symptoms of anxiety and stress. Additionally, the plant's root and bark are used to create a syrup that is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
side_effects: Ulmus × hollandica f. major can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of anthraquinones. Ingesting large amounts of this plant can also lead to allergic reactions, such as skin rashes and itching. Overconsumption of Ulmus × hollandica f. major may cause kidney damage and interact with certain medications, including blood thinners and diuretics. Furthermore, improper use may exacerbate existing conditions, such as kidney stones and digestive disorders.
